utility_notes: preserve positive-class Revenue=True coverage; purchase sessions are the minority class and are the key utility signal
PageValues is the strongest local purchase predictor and should be prioritized for utility and shape fidelity
ExitRates and BounceRates are important negative purchase indicators and must remain bounded and mutually coherent
ProductRelated and ProductRelated_Duration capture product-browsing depth and are important positive utility and trend anchors
Month, VisitorType, TrafficType, and Weekend carry session-context signal, but target Revenue must never appear in theta

pair_priors: bounce and exit rates are extremely strongly coupled bounded session-quality rates
product page count and product-page duration jointly describe browsing depth
informational page count and duration should stay coherent despite high zero mass
administrative page count and duration form a count-duration pair
sessions with more administrative interactions often also have deeper product browsing
product browsing depth and exit behavior are inversely related and utility-relevant
deeper product browsing generally corresponds to lower bounce behavior
longer product engagement should remain coherent with lower exit rates
high page value sessions usually have lower exit-rate patterns and stronger purchase signal
page value is tied to product-browsing depth and purchase likelihood
seasonality and proximity to special shopping days should remain coherent
visitor mix shifts by month and affects purchase-rate structure
acquisition channel and visitor type jointly describe session source
device and browser categories are related technical-session descriptors
region and traffic source can form rare session-context combinations
weekend sessions have a different visitor/purchase mix

shape: PageValues, duration columns, and product page counts are right-tailed; synthetic data can flatten high-value or long-session tails
PageValues, Informational, Informational_Duration, SpecialDay, and administrative fields are strongly zero-inflated; zero mass must be preserved
BounceRates and ExitRates are bounded rates in [0, 0.2]; values outside bounds or flattened central mass would distort shape
count-like columns Administrative, Informational, and ProductRelated should remain nonnegative and integer-like
rare categorical values in Browser, TrafficType, and OperatingSystems should not be dropped or amplified
trend: count-duration pairs must stay coherent: administrative, informational, and product-related page counts should align with their duration fields
BounceRates and ExitRates are the strongest pair dependency and should not be sampled independently
product-browsing depth should remain coherent with lower bounce/exit behavior and higher page values
Month and SpecialDay encode seasonal shopping context and should remain plausible together
acquisition/session context such as TrafficType, VisitorType, Weekend, browser, and region should not create impossible rare combinations
privacy: configured privacy columns are Administrative, Administrative_Duration, Informational, Informational_Duration, and ProductRelated
there is no direct human PII, but session behavior can be near-unique when count, duration, device, traffic, region, and timing context are combined
high-resolution duration and value fields can act as session fingerprints
rare TrafficType, Browser, and OperatingSystems categories can become strong nearest-neighbor handles
current code-derived bounds require broad col_ps for 17 features, but col_ps must not include all feature columns
utility: dropping PageValues will substantially weaken purchase classification utility
Revenue=True is the minority class at about 15.6%; synthetic data should preserve positive purchase-session coverage
using only device/browser/region categories as utility focus is too weak and can miss behavioral purchase signal
high BounceRates/ExitRates are negative purchase signals; preserving their inverse relation with purchase-like sessions matters
col_u should usually be PageValues, ExitRates, ProductRelated, BounceRates, or ProductRelated_Duration; never use the target column

seed_theta_examples.reason: preserves high-risk marginal shapes for page values, product browsing depth, bounded rates, zero-inflated special-day behavior, and configured session-behavior privacy columns while omitting device/browser/region from this privacy scope
focuses col_2ds on rate, count-duration, product-depth, and seasonal dependency groups; col_ps omits browser/region/weekend instead of copying the fidelity scope
broad DCR/privacy scope covers configured behavior columns plus value, rate, traffic, device, browser, and region fingerprints without using all features

uses the strongest purchase utility signal while retaining negative rate indicators, product browsing, season/traffic/visitor context, and a different privacy scope that omits informational/device fields
